title: Align madreperla.yaml to canonical shape
body:
## Summary

Fix madreperla.yaml field names and add missing path keys to match the canonical shape used across all madreperla-managed repos.

## Changes

Replace current madreperla.yaml contents with:

```yaml
description: "Automated vendor install control for repo-embedded tools via GitHub workflows"
docs: [AGENTS.md, README.md, docs/]
providers:
  issues:
    name: pearls
    config: "prl configure"
    usage: "prl docs"
  docs:
    name: repo
    usage: "cat docs/README.md"
    planning: docs/planning
    epics: docs/planning/epics
    ideas: docs/planning/ideas
    headaches: docs/planning/headaches
    retro: docs/planning
```

## Specific changes from current

- `providers.issues.docs` → `providers.issues.usage` (field rename)
- `providers.docs.docs` → `providers.docs.usage` (field rename)
- Add `planning: docs/planning`
- Add `epics: docs/planning/epics`
- Add `ideas: docs/planning/ideas`
- Add `headaches: docs/planning/headaches`
- Add `retro: docs/planning`
- Remove `designs` key if present

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] madreperla.yaml matches canonical shape above
- [ ] `docs/planning/ideas/` directory exists (create if missing)
- [ ] `docs/planning/headaches/` directory exists (create if missing)
- [ ] No `designs` key in config
- [ ] All `docs:` fields renamed to `usage:`

title: Create AGENTS.md with agent-facing instructions
body:
## Summary

Create a top-level AGENTS.md with concise, agent-facing instructions for working in this repo.

## Content guidelines

AGENTS.md should cover:
- What git-vendored is (one sentence)
- Repo structure overview (key directories)
- How to run tests
- Contributing conventions (commit style, PR process)
- Key commands agents need (install, check, remove, audit)
- Pointer to docs/ for deeper context

Keep it concise — this is a quick-reference for AI agents, not a full README.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] AGENTS.md exists at repo root
- [ ] Contains repo purpose, structure, test commands, and contributing conventions
- [ ] Under 80 lines
- [ ] No overlap/duplication with README.md content (complementary, not redundant)

title: Audit README.md for stale/inaccurate content
body:
## Summary

Scan the root README.md (278 lines) for stale or inaccurate content. The README was written during earlier phases and may reference removed features, outdated commands, or incorrect directory structures.

## Audit checklist

- Verify all referenced files/directories still exist
- Verify all CLI commands still work as documented
- Check that the "How It Works" section matches current behavior
- Check that environment variables match current install script
- Verify the bootstrap curl URL and version are current
- Check that the session-hook removal note is still accurate
- Verify config schema section matches current implementation
- Ensure migration instructions are still valid

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Every file path referenced in README.md verified against actual repo
- [ ] Every CLI command verified against actual scripts
- [ ] Stale/inaccurate content fixed or removed
- [ ] No references to removed features without clear deprecation context

title: Create docs/README.md table of contents
body:
## Summary

Create docs/README.md as a table of contents for the docs/ directory, listing each document with a brief description.

## Current docs/ contents

- `docs/adoption.md` — adoption guide
- `docs/vendor-install-dir-guide.md` — install dir guide
- `docs/planning/` — active planning
- `docs/planning/epics/` — epic planning docs

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] docs/README.md exists
- [ ] Lists all files and subdirectories in docs/ with one-line descriptions
- [ ] Includes brief intro sentence about what the docs directory contains
- [ ] Under 30 lines

title: README audit was shallow — only version URL fixed
body:
## Defect

The implementation of gv-d79b.3 only changed the bootstrap URL version from v0.1.0 to v0.0.28. The acceptance criteria required a comprehensive audit:

- [ ] Every file path referenced in README.md verified against actual repo
- [ ] Every CLI command verified against actual scripts
- [ ] Stale/inaccurate content fixed or removed
- [ ] No references to removed features without clear deprecation context

### Issues found during eval

1. The `.vendored/feedback` command exists in the repo but is not documented in README.md or AGENTS.md — unclear if this is intentional omission or oversight.
2. The audit checklist items (env var verification, config schema section, migration instructions) show no evidence of having been checked — only one line changed in the diff.
3. The acceptance criterion 'Every CLI command verified against actual scripts' has no evidence of verification beyond the version number.

### Linked issue
Defect of: gv-d79b.3

title: Docs cohesion: README.md, shipped-doc migration, config hygiene
